'''EUW:''' Cody Pog |checkboxAutoTeams=Yes |checkbox2=No |role=Bot |issub1=No |istrainee1=No |issub2=Yes |istrainee2=No |issub3=No |istrainee3=No |issub4=No |istrainee4=No |issub5=No |istrainee5=No |checkbox3=No |stream=https://www.twitch.tv/CodySun |facebook=https://facebook.com/CodySunLoL |twitter=CodySun |instagram=CodySunLoL |youtube=https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCG3IO5c7FAIVro3ohaqI9RA |discord=https://discord.gg/PcgKYDy |lolpros=https://lolpros.gg/player/cody-sun/ |deeplol=https://www.deeplol.gg/pro/Cody_Sun |checkboxSuppressOrgNavbox=No |checkboxSBS=Yes |checkboxTOCR=Yes |TOCLimit=2 |text=He was previously known as '''TM8''' and '''Massacre'''. |checkbox-res=No |checkboxIsSub=No |checkboxPrev=No }} ==Biography== ===2015 Season=== Cody Sun (born Sun Li-Yu) joined [[Magnetic]] in May 2015 and qualified for the [[2015 NA Challenger Series/Summer Season|NACS Summer Season]], losing to [[Misfits (North American Team)|Misfits]] and then beating [[CLG Black]] in the [[2015 NA Challenger Series/Summer Qualifier|qualifier]]. Prior to the start of the season, the team renamed to [[Team Imagine]]. Massacre played with Imagine for the first two weeks of the season before the entire roster was replaced. ===2016 Season=== Massacre played with [[Team Frostbite]] in the [[NA_Challenger_Series/2016_Season/Spring_Qualifiers/Open_Qualifier|NACS Open Qualifier]] and [[NA_Challenger_Series/2016_Season/Spring_Qualifiers|main qualifier]], but the team failed to make the [[NA_Challenger_Series/2016_Season/Spring_Season|Spring Season]]. After week 1 of the NACS, he joined [[Dream Team]], who had successfully qualified. Dream Team finished the season in fifth place, losing an automatic tiebreaker with [[Team Liquid Academy]] due to head-to-head record. They missed out on the [[NA Challenger Series/2016 Season/Spring Playoffs|playoffs]] but received higher seeding for the [[NA Challenger Series/2016 Season/Summer Qualifiers|summer qualifier]] due to finishing ahead of [[Enemy]]. Cody Sun left [[Dream Team]] in October and later in December joined [[Immortals]]. ===2017 Season=== In the [[League_Championship_Series/North_America/2017_Season/Spring_Season|Spring Split]] Immortals was for the most part a middle of the pack team. Although they showed promise they could never break into the Top 3. Cody Sun's performance in particular was very rough and he was widely criticized for his inexperience and subpar play. The team finished in 7th place, barely missing out on the playoffs. In the [[League_Championship_Series/North_America/2017_Season/Summer_Season|Summer Split]] after bringing in [[SSONG]] as their new coach, Immortals' performance turned around completely and they stayed in the Top 3 throughout the whole regular split. Cody Sun was praised for his improved play and synergy with his support [[Olleh]]. The team finished the regular season in 2nd place with a 14-4 record. Their performance in the [[League_Championship_Series/North_America/2017_Season/Summer_Playoffs|Summer Playoffs]] was also excellent as they took down [[Counter Logic Gaming]] 3-0 in the semifinals and became the first team in NA LCS history since 2013 outside of CLG, TSM and C9 to reach the finals. Despite their accomplishment however, they were defeated 1-3 by TSM in the finals and finished 2nd place. Despite the loss they still managed to qualify for the [[2017 Season World Championship]] via championship points. At Worlds Immortals was seeded in Group B along with [[Longzhu Gaming]], [[Fnatic]] and [[GIGABYTE Marines]]. Despite a good 2-1 start in Week 1 they couldn't follow up on it in Week 2 and went 0-3. Having to play a tiebreaker in order to advance forward, Immortals faced Fnatic and lost the game which knocked them out of Worlds. Due to Immortals not being accepted into the newly franchised NA LCS, Cody Sun's contract was sold to [[Team Liquid]] in November. However, Liquid's subsequent acquisition of [[Doublelift]] made him redundant, and so he joined the recently created [[100 Thieves]] in December of 2017. == Trivia == * Born in China but has Canadian citizenship.[https://twitter.com/CodySun/status/1058816022818353152 Cody Sun's Tweet] ''twitter.com'' ** Nicknamed "孙哥", translating to "Brother Sun", by Chinese fans.[https://youtu.be/o7JxsPe0x3o Chinese nicknames for Western LOL pro players] ''youtube.com'' * Infamously flashed into four members of [[Fnatic]] in the group stage of [[2017 Season World Championship/Main Event|2017 Worlds]], giving Fnatic their first win and eventually leading to them overtaking [[Immortals]] and making it out of the group. * Had a seven game deathless streak stretching through the last five games of the [[LCS/2019 Season/Summer Season|LCS 2019 Summer Season]] and the first two games of the [[LCS/2019 Season/Summer Playoffs|LCS 2019 Summer Playoffs]], before finally dying in game 3 of the quarterfinals against [[TSM]]. * Considered extremely underrated by many NA casters and analysts, as he was never considered one of the best NA bot laners, despite excellent statistics, and was sometimes not even the starter on his teams. ** As a result he has reached the [[World Championship]] in each of his first three years in the LCS, but with three different teams, and was benched by [[100 Thieves]] for the entire [[2018 Season World Championship]]. * Known for playing hyper-scaling bot laners like {{ci|Xayah}} and {{ci|Kai'sa}}. == Tournament Results == {{PlayerResults|show=overviewpage}} {{PlayerShowmatchResults|show=overviewpage}} {{PlayerResults1v1|show=overviewpage}} ==Media== {{PlayerMedia}} === Images === {{PlayerProfileGallery}} {{PlayerPageEnd}}
